title: NHRC Issues Heatwave Warning as Telangana Records 46°C; No Advisory Issued Yet - Deccan Chronicle
full_text:
NHRC Issues Heatwave Warning as Telangana Records 46°C; No Advisory Issued Yet
On Thursday, maximum temperatures in Telangana touched 46°C in Adilabad, with several districts including Kamareddy, Nirmal, Jagtial and Kumuram Bheem recording between 45.8°C and 45.9°C.
Hyderabad: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has listed Telangana among nearly 20 states asked to take urgent steps to protect people from intensifying heat, as temperatures across the state remain high without a fresh public advisory.
On Thursday, maximum temperatures in Telangana touched 46°C in Adilabad, with several districts including Kamareddy, Nirmal, Jagtial and Kumuram Bheem recording between 45.8°C and 45.9°C. Most other districts remained in the 42°C to 45°C range, indicating sustained heat stress conditions.
Despite this, the state’s public health and family welfare (DPH&FW) department has not issued a new heatwave advisory.
Speaking to Deccan Chronicle, Dr B. Ravinder Naik, director of the department said: “An advisory was in place and an updated one will be released soon.” The last advisory, however, was issued nearly three weeks ago.
The Telangana Development Planning Society (TGDPS) has identified heatwave conditions in multiple mandals over the past two days, based on temperatures crossing 45°C.
“In the absence of a clear heatwave advisory, people often underestimate the risk and delay basic precautions like hydration, avoiding peak hours, or seeking medical help,” said Dr S Karunesha, a consultant general physician. “That can quickly lead to heat exhaustion or even heatstroke, especially among children, the elderly and outdoor workers.”
In Hyderabad, temperatures continued to stay above 41°C across all circles, with peaks touching 42.5°C in areas such as Hayathnagar and Secunderabad.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecast that high temperatures will persist for the next few days, with only isolated thunderstorms offering limited relief until around May 3-4.

title: Heatwave And Thunderstorm Alert Issued For Telangana - INDToday
full_text:
Hyderabad, April 30: Heatwave and thunderstorm alert in Telangana remains in force as temperatures continue to soar above 40 degree Celsius across the state.
The India Meteorological Department has warned that intense heat will persist despite chances of scattered rainfall.
Heatwave And Thunderstorm Alert In Telangana
Adilabad recorded the highest temperature at 44.3 degree Celsius on Wednesday.
Nizamabad followed with 44.2 degree Celsius, while Medak and Khammam crossed 42 degree Celsius.
Mahabubnagar recorded 41.9 degree Celsius, indicating widespread heatwave conditions.
In Hyderabad, temperatures stayed above 40 degree Celsius under an orange alert.
Hayathnagar was the hottest at 41 degree Celsius, followed by Hakimpet, Rajendra Nagar and Begumpet.
The IMD has forecast isolated thunderstorms with gusty winds in parts of Hyderabad, Medchal Malkajgiri, Rangareddy and Sangareddy.
These weather changes are likely on Thursday evening or early Friday.
However, officials said there will be no major drop in temperatures over the next three days.
The heatwave is expected to intensify further by May 3.
Temperatures in Hyderabad and nearby districts may range between 41 and 44 degree Celsius.
Authorities advised people to take precautions and avoid prolonged exposure to heat.

title: Intense heatwave and thunderstorm alert grips Telangana; Scattered rains forecast - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Hyderabad: Amid the intense summer heat, Adilabad sizzled at 44.3 degree Celsius topping the high temperatures list of Telangana on Wednesday.
Day temperatures remained high across most parts of the state with Nizamabad recording 44.2 degree Celsius, Medak logging in 42.6 degree Celsius, Khammam with 42.4 degree Celsius and and Mahabubnagar recording 41.9 degree Celsius.
The grueling heatwave continues to dominate with maximum temperatures soaring past 44 degree celsius in northern districts and 40 degree Celsius in Hyderabad.
While the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad, has forecast isolated thunderstorms, the mercury is expected to remain high in several regions.
Hyderabad remains under orange alert as temperatures hover above 40 degree Celsius. In the last 24 hours, Hayathnagar emerged as the hottest region at 41 degree Celsius followed by Hakimpet (40.8) Rajendra Nagar (40.5), Begumpet (40.4) and Patancheru (39.4).
The IMD, Hyderabad forecast indicated that isolated places in Hyderabad, Medchal-Malkajgiri, Rangareddy, and Sangareddy are likely to experience thunderstorms and gusty winds on Thursday evening or early hours of Friday.
Despite the potential for localized rain, the IMD warns that there will be no large change in maximum temperatures for the next three days. Looking ahead to May 3, the heatwave conditions are projected to intensify further, with temperatures in Hyderabad and surrounding districts expected to range between 41 degree Celsius and 44 degree Celsius.

title: Telangana Weather Alert: Heatwave Hits 46°C, Thunderstorms & Unseasonal Rains Predicted - Munsif News 24x7
full_text:
Hyderabad: Telangana is facing extreme heatwave conditions along with sudden thunderstorms and unseasonal rains, with temperatures touching 46°C in Nirmal district. According to officials and the Hyderabad Meteorological Centre, light to moderate rains, gusty winds, and cloudy skies are expected in several districts, while heatwave conditions continue across the state. The situation is ongoing, with warnings issued for the next two days.
Table of Contents
Telangana Heatwave Intensifies with Record Temperatures
The heatwave has intensified across Telangana:
- 45.8°C at Dhanora in Kumuram Bheem Asifabad district
- 45.8°C at Alipur (Raikal mandal) in Jagtial district
By 11 AM, roads across towns in Telangana were reported largely deserted due to intense heat.
Districts severely affected by heatwave include:
Thunderstorms and Unseasonal Rains Add to Weather Crisis
Despite extreme heat, Telangana is also witnessing:
Forecast details:
- Light to moderate rain expected in Vikarabad, Sangareddy, Kamareddy, Asifabad, and Medak districts on Thursday
- Cloudy conditions likely in Rangareddy and Nalgonda districts in the evening
- Hyderabad Meteorological Centre predicts light to moderate rain in Khammam, Nalgonda, and Warangal districts on Friday and Saturday
These weather changes are caused by air instability due to high temperatures.
Impact on Farmers and Crops
Farmers are facing serious difficulties:
- Mango and chilli crops damaged due to unseasonal rains
- Drying grain stocks getting wet due to sudden showers
The unpredictable mix of heatwave and rainfall is causing significant agricultural losses.
Public Advisory Issued Amid Severe Heat
Officials have issued warnings:
Safety during thunderstorms:
- Do not take shelter under trees during lightning
- Farmers and cattle herders advised to remain cautious
Current Status
- Government and weather officials monitoring situation
Telangana is experiencing a severe heatwave with temperatures up to 46°C along with thunderstorms and unseasonal rains, affecting districts like Nirmal, Nizamabad, Adilabad, Siddipet, and Nalgonda. With rain forecasts for multiple districts and ongoing heatwave warnings, the situation remains critical, and authorities have urged people to take precautions.
